input.background: Exercise 17.4 considered a randomized trial of smoking cessation in which treatment with bupropion in combination with a nicotine patch was compared to use of a nicotine patch alone. The treatment group had success in 87 of 245 participants. The control group had successes in 40 of 244 participants.
input.question: Why is this confidence interval more useful than the hypothesis test results calculated in Exercise 17.4?

output.answer: What useful information do these statistics add...? The size of the effect is estimated. (The hypothesis test merely addressed chance as an explanation for any effect.)

treatment_success_count.description: Number of successful smoking cessation cases in the treatment group.

treatment_total_count.description: Total number of participants in the treatment group.

control_success_count.description: Number of successful smoking cessation cases in the control group.

control_total_count.description: Total number of participants in the control group.
